alerts.title: Trump weighs fresh strikes on Iran as Tehran says deal 'not imminent'
alerts.summary: The New York Times reports Trump is actively weighing options for new strikes inside Iran, while BBC quotes Iran stating any US agreement is 'not imminent' — directly contradicting Trump's earlier 'largely negotiated' framing. A senior US official also told Fox News (via ME Spectator, ~41K views) no deal will be signed 'today or tomorrow,' citing a 'No Dust, No Dollars' posture, indicating the Hormuz framework is fraying as threat level holds at 9/CRITICAL.

alerts.title: IDF chief urges cabinet to strike Beirut buildings over Hezbollah drone attacks
alerts.summary: The Jerusalem Post reports Israel's IDF chief told the security cabinet that Israel must strike buildings inside Beirut in response to recent Hezbollah drone activity — a significant threshold beyond current south-Lebanon strike patterns. The recommendation comes as Hezbollah remains flagged 'Active' in proxy metrics and risks reopening a second front while US-Iran talks falter.

alerts.title: Houthi missile strike on Israel revives Red Sea shipping threat
alerts.summary: AP News and The Times of Israel report a fresh Houthi missile attack on Israel has stoked fears of renewed strikes on Red Sea commercial shipping, with BBC and Global Security Review publishing parallel threat assessments. Houthi status in proxy metrics is 'Elevated — Multiple Recent Attacks'; USS Eisenhower, Bataan, Carney, Mason and Laboon are all concentrated in the southern Red Sea around 15-16°N near the Bab el-Mandeb.

alerts.title: Khamenei: enriched uranium must stay in Iran; Tehran rejects nuclear commitments
alerts.summary: Reuters exclusive reports Supreme Leader Khamenei has ruled that Iran's enriched uranium stockpile must remain inside the country, while Iran's semi-official Tasnim (via ME Spectator, ~46K views) states Tehran has made 'zero commitments on nuclear issues' and demands at least half of frozen assets be released up front. With enrichment at 60% U-235 and breakout estimated at 1-2 weeks, the public hardening directly contradicts US claims of an 'understanding' on the stockpile.

alerts.title: US deploys defensive 'umbrella' of tech and assets over Strait of Hormuz shipping
alerts.summary: DefenseScoop reports the US is fielding a layered defense and technology 'umbrella' to shield commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, amid widespread GPS jamming across the Middle East (The Week) and Iran-linked hacker activity targeting US interests (PBS). USS Florida (SSGN) remains positioned in the Persian Gulf at ~25.5°N, 51.8°E, consistent with sustained deterrent posture as Brent holds at $100.21.

analysis_note: The US-Iran framework is visibly fracturing — Trump is weighing renewed strikes while Tehran publicly rejects nuclear commitments and hardens on uranium custody — even as Hezbollah, Houthi, and Hormuz pressure points simultaneously escalate.
